title: Wormia obovata
taxonomy: Wormia obovata is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae and the tribe Willughbieae. The classification of Wormia obovata is further broken down into the genus Wormia and the species W. obovata. This plant is native to tropical Africa and is part of a diverse group of plants with unique characteristics. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally agreed upon that Wormia obovata belongs to the above-mentioned family and subfamily.

intro: Wormia obovata, also known as Chinese wormwood, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. Traditionally, Chinese wormwood is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Wormia obovata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Wormia obovata is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to treat various health conditions.
